relock_reason: The v1.5 collector failed closed during golden-only development before any detector execution, and its untouched sibling remains unexecuted but is conservatively withdrawn. Version 1.6 binds the complete historical exclusion union and the corrected pre-source detector-independent collector contract before fresh metadata-only cohort selection.
eligibility: publicly cloneable repository at freeze time
full immutable Git commit can be resolved
contains an observable LLM application, agent, RAG, evaluation, gateway, or orchestration surface
primary implementation is TypeScript/JavaScript or Python, or exposes those integrations
not a fork or near-duplicate of another selected repository
inspection is permitted by published repository terms
can be scanned within 4 GiB checkout size and 30 minute wall-clock ceiling
exclusion: documentation-only repository with no runnable integration surface
model weights or dataset repository without application code
archived repository with no uniquely needed coverage stratum
repository present in the exact historical exclusion ledger
repository selected for another current cohort
repository chosen or replaced because of observed detector performance
assignment: Two new context-isolated metadata-only searches will propose candidates before source access or detector output. The deterministic constraint selector will produce fresh, mutually disjoint 24-repository golden and untouched cohorts after excluding the exact historical ledger, including every v1.5 candidate and proposal identity. Assignment is not random and any results must not be generalized to all LLM applications.
replacement_rule: Use the first eligible reserve candidate with the same primary surface stratum; record the failed eligibility clause and never consult detector output.
reserve_extension_rule: If every preregistered reserve for a required surface is consumed or ineligible, append and record the first metadata-eligible same-surface reserve, then re-lock and independently review the complete policy and cohorts before any detector execution.
blinding_incident_rule: If untouched source opportunities or labels reach a rule author before the detector artifact is formally frozen, retire the entire affected cohort from release measurement. Preserve immutable artifacts, disclose the incident, select a completely disjoint replacement from two independent metadata-only searches, bind the exact exclusion set and deterministic selector, and independently review and re-freeze both current manifests before any detector execution.
replacement_selection_rule: Select exactly gateway=1, chat_app=3, local_model=3, rag=4, agent_tools=6, evaluation_or_framework=7 while requiring Anthropic, OpenAI, and local/open-model provider coverage plus at least four TypeScript/JavaScript and eight Python repositories. Use deterministic dynamic constraint selection, ranking exact dual-proposal agreement before single-proposal candidates and then SHA-256 of policy_id|surface|lowercase_repository. Exclude classification conflicts rather than resolving them after selection. Golden must exclude the exact historical ledger; untouched must additionally bind and exclude the exact golden candidate and selection record. The selected cohort source must not be read before the policy, candidate documents, selection records, and immutable manifests are public and reviewed.
